title: rubus serpens subsp. humorosus
intro: Rubus serpens subsp. humorosus, also known as the creeping raspberry, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its astringent and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Rubus serpens subsp. humorosus include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
